summary: Recent updates to Firefox's negotiation engine, specifically the JSEP (JavaScript Session Establishment Protocol), enhance its handling of audio in video calls using the Opus codec, a popular choice for its quality and efficiency. The patches ensure that the parameters related to packet time (ptime), minimum packet time (minptime), and maximum packet time (maxptime) are accurately parsed and applied from the SIPCC (Session Initiation Protocol Communications Client) SDP (Session Description Protocol) parser. This improvement matters because it enables better control over audio stream qualities, resulting in clearer calls and reduced latency, ultimately leading to an enhanced user experience in real-time communication.
